description: End-to-end user experience testing for the bowls tournament feature — tournament listing, creation, check-in, board, draw generation, kiosk mode, and navigation

userStories.title: Tournament list page displays active and past bowls tournaments
userStories.description: As a bowler arriving at the club, I need to see all available lawn bowling tournaments on /bowls so I can pick the one I want to check into today.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /bowls returns 200 and renders the tournament list page
Page header shows 'Lawn Bowls' title and 'Tournament check-in & draw management' subtitle
'+ New Tournament' button is visible in the header
Active tournaments section shows tournaments with status != 'completed' with Active badge
Past tournaments section shows completed tournaments with Done badge
Each tournament card displays: name, format label, checked-in count, creator name
Tapping a tournament card navigates to /bowls/[id]
Empty state shows bowling emoji, 'No bowls tournaments yet' message, and create button
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Files: src/app/bowls/page.tsx. Test with and without existing tournaments. Verify the Supabase query filters by sport='lawn_bowling'.

userStories.title: Create new bowls tournament via modal
userStories.description: As a tournament organizer, I need to create a new lawn bowling tournament with a name, format, max players, and start date so players can check in for today's social bowls.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking '+ New Tournament' opens the CreateBowlsTournamentModal
Modal contains fields: Tournament Name (text), Format (4 buttons: Fours/Triples/Pairs/Singles), Max Players (number, default 32), Start Date (datetime-local)
Format buttons use blue highlight when selected, default selection is 'Fours'
Submitting with a name POSTs to /api/bowls/tournament with correct payload
API creates tournament with sport='lawn_bowling' and format matching selected bowls format
After creation, modal closes and tournament list refreshes showing the new tournament
Empty name shows validation — submit button is disabled
Error state displays red error message in the modal
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Files: src/components/bowls/CreateBowlsTournamentModal.tsx, src/app/api/bowls/tournament/route.ts. Verify Radix Dialog accessibility (escape to close, focus trap).

userStories.title: Tournament check-in flow — search player and select position
userStories.description: As a lawn bowler arriving for a tournament, I need to find my name in the player list, tap it, and choose my preferred position (Skip/Vice/Second/Lead) to register for today's draw.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /bowls/[id] loads the check-in view by default with search bar and player grid
Search bar filters players by display_name as user types
Player grid shows 2 columns on mobile, up to 5 on desktop
Unchecked players show gradient avatar with initials
Tapping an unchecked player opens the position selection modal
Position modal shows player name, format label, and position buttons with descriptions
Each position button shows: colored circle with order number, position name, description
Position colors are consistent: Skip=blue, Vice=purple, Second=amber, Lead=emerald
Selecting a position POSTs to /api/bowls/checkin with player_id, tournament_id, preferred_position
After check-in, player card shows position badge with ring effect and green checkmark animation
Tapping a checked-in player calls DELETE /api/bowls/checkin to undo
Header shows tournament name and live count of registered players
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Files: src/app/bowls/[id]/page.tsx, src/app/api/bowls/checkin/route.ts. Touch targets must be min 44px. Position modal buttons are min 72px. Test the 5-second polling for real-time updates.

userStories.title: Board view shows position summary, stats, and checked-in players
userStories.description: As a drawmaster managing the tournament, I need to see how many players are checked in at each position so I can decide when to generate the draw.
acceptanceCriteria: Switching to 'Board' tab shows position summary cards, stats bar, and player list
Position summary shows 4 cards (for fours format): Skip, Vice, Second, Lead with counts
Each card has the correct position color and description text
Stats bar shows total players checked in (left), possible rinks count (right), and Generate Draw button
Generate Draw button is disabled when possibleRinks < 1
Checked-in player list shows each player with position-colored avatar, name, and position badge
Format selector in header changes position cards shown (e.g. Pairs shows only Skip and Lead)
Rink count calculation is correct: floor(checkins / (playersPerTeam * 2))
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Files: src/app/bowls/[id]/page.tsx (board view section). Verify math: 8 players in fours = 1 rink, 7 players = 0 rinks.

userStories.title: Draw generation produces correct rink assignments
userStories.description: As a drawmaster, I need to generate a fair draw that assigns checked-in players to rinks based on their preferred positions, with two balanced teams per rink.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking 'Generate Draw' POSTs to /api/bowls/draw with tournament_id and format
View switches to Draw tab showing rink cards
Each rink card has a header with rink number and two columns: Team 1 and Team 2
Players in each team are sorted by position order (Skip first, Lead last)
Each player row shows a position badge (colored) and player name
Position badges use consistent colors (Skip=blue, Vice=purple, Second=amber, Lead=emerald)
Unassigned players section appears in amber when players don't fit into complete rinks
Re-Draw button generates a fresh draw with new shuffle
Draw header shows format label and rink count
The draw engine respects position preferences — players get their preferred position when possible
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Files: src/app/bowls/[id]/page.tsx (draw view), src/lib/bowls-draw.ts, src/app/api/bowls/draw/route.ts. Test with exactly 8 players (1 full rink in fours), 9 players (1 rink + 1 unassigned), and edge cases.

userStories.title: Kiosk mode wraps bowls pages for iPad fullscreen
userStories.description: As a club volunteer setting up the iPad kiosk, I need the bowls pages to work in fullscreen kiosk mode with inactivity timeout and admin access.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /kiosk/bowls returns 200 and shows the tournament list in KioskWrapper
GET /kiosk/bowls/[id] returns 200 and shows the check-in page in KioskWrapper
KioskWrapper provides 60-second inactivity timeout that resets the view
Triple-tap top-right corner opens admin PIN modal
Both kiosk routes are touch-optimized with large tap targets
Pages render correctly in iPad landscape dimensions (1024x768)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Files: src/app/kiosk/bowls/page.tsx, src/app/kiosk/bowls/[id]/page.tsx, src/components/kiosk/KioskWrapper.tsx. Test on iPad viewport.

userStories.title: BottomNav includes Bowls link and navigates correctly
userStories.description: As a player using the app on my phone, I need to see a Bowls icon in the bottom navigation bar so I can quickly get to the lawn bowls section.
acceptanceCriteria: BottomNav shows 5 items: Home, Bowls, Schedule, Stats, Me
Bowls nav item uses the bowling emoji icon
Tapping Bowls navigates to /bowls
Active indicator (green bar) shows on Bowls when on /bowls route
Nav is hidden on desktop (lg breakpoint)
Each nav item has minimum 44px touch target
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Files: src/components/board/BottomNav.tsx. Verify the active state detection works for both /bowls and /bowls/[id] routes.

userStories.title: Bowls tournament API handles CRUD operations correctly
userStories.description: As the system, the bowls tournament API needs to correctly create and list lawn bowling tournaments with proper authentication and validation.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/bowls/tournament returns tournaments filtered by sport='lawn_bowling'
GET response includes creator display_name and participant count
POST /api/bowls/tournament creates tournament with sport='lawn_bowling'
POST validates required fields: name and bowls_format
POST validates bowls_format is one of: fours, triples, pairs, singles
POST returns 400 for invalid bowls_format
POST returns 401 for unauthenticated requests
POST returns 404 if player record not found
Successful POST returns 201 with tournament data
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Files: src/app/api/bowls/tournament/route.ts. Test both success and error paths.

userStories.title: Seed script creates demo bowls tournament with check-ins
userStories.description: As a developer setting up a demo environment, I need the seed script to create a bowls tournament and sample check-ins so the bowls pages aren't empty on first load.
acceptanceCriteria: Running npx tsx scripts/seed-demo.ts creates a 'Saturday Social Bowls' tournament
Tournament has sport='lawn_bowling', format='fours', max_players=32
6 demo players are checked in with varied positions (2 skips, 2 vices, 1 second, 1 lead)
Seed is idempotent — running twice doesn't create duplicates
--clean flag removes bowls tournament and check-ins
Step counters show 9 total steps
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Files: scripts/seed-demo.ts. Bowls seeding is wrapped in try/catch for resilience if bowls_checkins table doesn't exist.

userStories.title: Migration script for bowls_checkins table
userStories.description: As a developer deploying to production, I need a migration script that creates the bowls_checkins table with proper schema, indexes, and RLS policies.
acceptanceCriteria: Script reads SQL from src/lib/db/migrations/006_bowls_checkins.sql
Script uses Supabase service role client from .env.local
bowls_checkins table has columns: id (uuid PK), player_id (FK), tournament_id (FK), preferred_position (check constraint), checked_in_at
Unique constraint on (player_id, tournament_id)
RLS policies allow: select for all, insert/update/delete for all
Realtime publishing enabled
Script verifies table exists after migration
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Files: scripts/migration-bowls-checkins.ts, src/lib/db/migrations/006_bowls_checkins.sql. Note: exec_sql RPC may not be available, script includes fallback.
